disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden episode tonight of confusion and disorientation — did not recognise the carer for about ten minutes
trigger_question_examples: How were they behaving during the episode?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Out of nowhere they got very confused — they looked straight at me and didn't know who I was for about ten minutes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Transient difficulty producing speech during the episode — halting, stuck for words; now resolved
trigger_question_examples: Were they able to speak normally?
What was their speech like during the episode?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They couldn't get a sentence out — really halting, stuck on words. It's back to normal now, just a bit slow.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Currently on active chemotherapy (fourth cycle) for lymphoma
trigger_question_examples: Are they having any treatment at the moment?
Do they have any medical conditions?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They've got lymphoma and they're partway through chemo — this is their fourth round.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Background of roughly two months of gradually worsening short-term memory and difficulty multitasking
trigger_question_examples: Has anything like this been building up?
How has their memory been over recent weeks?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's been foggy memory and trouble juggling tasks for a couple of months, but nothing anywhere near this bad.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A safety event during the episode — left the gas stove running and was found wandering the hallway
trigger_question_examples: Was anyone or anything put at risk during the episode?
What were they doing when you found them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They'd left the gas on and I found them wandering the hallway — that's what scared me most.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever measured on a home thermometer and none noticed
trigger_question_examples: Have they had any fever, sweats or chills?
Have you been able to take their temperature?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever that we've noticed — the home thermometer read 36.8.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Carer did not observe any facial drooping or one-sided limb weakness; speech has returned to normal
trigger_question_examples: Did you notice any drooping of the face or weakness on one side?
Is their face or body moving normally now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I didn't see any face droop or arm weakness, and they're speaking normally again now.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'm not medically trained though — I was panicking, so I can't be sure I'd have picked up something subtle.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No head injury or fall, and no sudden severe headache or vomiting reported
trigger_question_examples: Any bump to the head or fall?
Any sudden severe headache or vomiting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No falls or head knocks, and they haven't complained of a bad headache or been sick.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No sedating medicines and no recent changes to the medication regimen
trigger_question_examples: Any new medicines recently, or anything that makes them drowsy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No new tablets and nothing sedating — the chemo and the usual anti-sickness and pain tablets are unchanged.
